h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange proceeds linearly without a pivot turn to rupture the forward trajectory.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: One bar equals three quarter-note beats, so those two cards fill the blanks. The speed card does not fit either blank; it needs a margin label, or it still looks like a third box waiting to be filled.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the tutor's new fill-in-the-blank test to physically sort the cards into the correct conceptual zones, visibly completing the new device.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The arrows settled the two boxes; they did not decide the loose speed card. Use one test now: only a label that can complete one bar = ___ ___ beats enters the time signature.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges the failure of the previous arrow-mapping to resolve the speed card and introduces a new syntactic fill-in-the-blank test to force a categorical sorting.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to trigger a recognition-contingent adaptation from the tutor.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn to trigger a recognition-contingent adaptation from the tutor.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges the failure of the previous arrow-mapping to resolve the speed card and introduces a new syntactic fill-in-the-blank test to force a categorical sorting.

scored.recoheredEarlier: the left arrow from the table contact, right?
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will simply place the friction card on the left arrow and accept the table contact label as its unique identifier without further complication.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Friction goes in the strip slot. Normal goes in the upright slot. Gravity stays out of both table slots; it should wait for the downward arrow from Earth on cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the newly introduced surface key device to correctly classify and spatially place the remaining force cards.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The contact mark no longer sorts the two table forces; table on cart is shared. Use this surface key instead: one slot runs along the tabletop strip, one slot stands off it at the square.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges the failure of the previous sorting rule and introduces a new physical representation to resolve the ambiguity.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The contact mark no longer sorts the two table forces; table on cart is shared. Use this surface key instead: one slot runs along the tabletop strip, one slot stands off it at the square.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly validates the learner's revised framing about the shared contact point and shifts the task to a new spatial sorting mechanism in response.
